channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Lawyers for Sean 'Diddy' Combs say sex acts are distorted by 'sexist and puritanical' prosecution
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs labeled the prosecution against the hip-hop star as “sexist and puritanical” on Tuesday, saying nine recordings of sex acts considered important evidence in the case show only consensual sex between willing adults.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: LA family used ‘budget friendly’ system pumping water from pool to help save their home from wildfires
item.description: Homeowners in Palisades Highlands in Los Angeles County showed on social media how they used a pump system with their swimming pool to help save their home from wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New wildfire warnings issued and more power is shut off as winds rise in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Millions of people across Southern California faced new wildfire warnings Tuesday, and tens of thousands saw their power shut off as strong winds blew across the parched landscape around Los Angeles where two massive blazes have been burning for a week.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Missing persons: New Ohio task force to reunite families with loved ones
item.description: Ohio is taking a significant step to change how missing persons cases are handled. Governor Mike DeWine has announced the formation of a new task force designed to improve investigations and reunite families with their loved ones.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: The Freedom Caucus takes control of the Wyoming House, marking its first chance to lead
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — As President-elect Donald Trump plans bold moves for his first days in office, so too are conservative lawmakers in Wyoming, the first state where Trump-friendly Freedom Caucus members have won control of a statehouse chamber.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chemicals in sewage sludge fertilizer pose cancer risk, EPA says
item.description: Harmful chemicals in sewage sludge that is spread on pasture land as fertilizer are causing cancer, the Environmental Protection Agency said Tuesday. The risk is highest for people who regularly consume milk, beef and other products from farms where it is spread.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Speaker Johnson orders US Capitol flags raised to full height for Trump's inauguration
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson on Tuesday ordered that flags at the U.S. Capitol be raised to their full height on Inauguration Day, pausing a 30-day flag-lowering order following the death of former President Jimmy Carter.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: More firefighters rush to Los Angeles ahead of stronger winds
item.description: A growing force of firefighters and equipment moved into the Los Angeles area as another round of powerful winds threatened to trigger new wildfires
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Federal prosecutors won't seek charges in deadly arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene
item.description: Federal prosecutors told family members Tuesday they will not bring charges in the deadly 2019 arrest of Black motorist Ronald Greene, closing the books on a lengthy FBI investigation into the white troopers who stunned, punched and dragged Greene on a roadside and allegations of an attempted cover-
item.dc:creator: JIM MUSTIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the devastation from the Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fires burning homes and businesses in Los Angeles for a week have killed at least 24 people, displaced thousands of others and destroyed more than 12,000 buildings in what might be the most expensive set of conflagrations in the nation's history. The blazes started Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Florida bill would ban students in the US without legal permission from some public universities
item.description: A Florida state lawmaker has filed a bill that would ban some of the state’s public colleges and universities from admitting students who are in the country without legal permission.
item.dc:creator: Kate Payne, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: REAL ID enforcement may be phased, but deadline is the same: What to know
item.description: The REAL ID deadline is fast-approaching and officials want you to be prepared (spoiler: you may already be in compliance).
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Bourbon Street terrorist researched Germany Christmas market attack, Mardi Gras before deadly rampage: FBI
item.description: The FBI has released a new photo of New Orleans terrorist attacker Shamsud-Din Jabbar as they continue to investigate what motivated his New Year's attack on Bourbon Street.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USDA documented insects and slime at Boar's Head plants, records show
item.description: Government inspectors documented unsanitary conditions at several Boar's Head deli meat plants, not just the factory that was shut down last year after a deadly outbreak of listeria poisoning, federal records show.
item.dc:creator: JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Michelle Obama to skip Trump inauguration
item.description: Former first lady Michelle Obama is skipping President-elect Trump's inauguration next week, after raising eyebrows by not attending former President Jimmy Carter's funeral in Washington, D.C., last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Trump claims California had the chance to 'maintain their forests' amid Los Angeles wildfires
item.description: “The problem is it's so dry, it was always so dry there, and it's just … it's just a mess. They could’ve, you know, maintained their forests, and — as I said they have to do,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats' hopes of derailing Trump nominees fading fast
item.description: Democrats' hopes of defeating any of President-elect Trump's Cabinet nominees appear to be fizzling as Senate committees prepare for the first week of hearings.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Senate GOP incumbents brace for MAGA primary challengers
item.description: Several GOP Senate incumbents are already staring down the threat of primary challenges next year as friction builds between the MAGA and establishment wings. Sen.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Princess Kate says her cancer is in remission
item.description: The Princess of Wales revealed Tuesday that her cancer is in remission after an emotional visit to the hospital where she received treatment last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rashida Jones is stepping down as MSNBC president on the eve of Trump inauguration
item.description: MSNBC President Rashida Jones says she is stepping down after four years leading the liberal news network, her move coming on the eve of a second Trump administration and after changes in corporate ownership.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Girls basketball coach accused of hitting person with car after argument
item.description: A girls' youth basketball coach is accused of hitting a person with his vehicle after an argument following a game Saturday in Columbia, according to state police, and it was caught on surveillance video.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge approves emergency order to close migrant gang-infested Aurora, Colorado, apartment complex
item.description: An Aurora, Colorado, judge ordered the closure of the Edge at Lowry apartments on Tuesday, a complex at the center of violent gang crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Al Capone called it his sweetheart. The pistol that saved his life is coming to Vegas
item.description: A rare glimpse into mobster Al Capone’s home life will be on display in a new exhibit at the Mob Museum in downtown Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: Rio Yamat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Live updates: Democrats turn up heat on Hegseth as Republican alleges hypocrisy
item.description: Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth appeared before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Tuesday, the first of President-elect Trump's Cabinet candidates to face confirmation.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Small airborne embers play a big role in the spread of wildfires
item.description: While authorities still don’t know what sparked the deadly fires in the Los Angeles area, they do know one clear way the flames have spread: embers. At least 24 people have died in the fires that have destroyed more than 12,000 structures since starting last Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maquiladora sites to help Mexican border town shelter deported migrants
item.description: The Mexican town of Piedras Negras, across from Eagle Pass, Texas, is readying plans to open warehouses formerly used as maquiladoras, if needed, to house and help deported migrants if the incoming Trump administration sends masses south of the border.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Firefighters deploy as strong winds threaten more LA neighborhoods
item.description: A week after the first fires began, the flames have destroyed thousands of homes and killed at least 24 people.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: 153 winners of Nobel and World Food prizes seek new ways to grow food to meet surging global need
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — More than 150 recipients of the Nobel and World Food prizes released an open letter Tuesday calling for a dramatic increase in research and a commitment to new food distribution efforts with a goal of producing more crops and avoiding a global hunger crisis in coming decades.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Multiracial boom in 2020 census was mostly an illusion, researchers say
item.description: When the 2020 census results were released, they showed a boom in the number of people classified as multiracial in the United States since 2010. Two Princeton sociologists now say that jump was mostly an illusion.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles price gouging is 'inhumane': Real estate agent
item.description: Nikko Santo Pietro, Los Angeles real estate agent and son of 'Wheel of Fortune’s' Vanna White, said the supply of housing is “so limited.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Winter weather road tips: Staying safe while navigating the snow and ice
item.description: CHICAGO (WGN) — Snowfall brings a whole bundle of headaches for drivers, but there are some steps that can be taken to avoid lingering issues or a potentially dangerous situation.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, ABC 4
